findings.evidence: docs/vocabulary.md defines 'ScoreReport' as the canonical term for 'the output of scoring an EvalRunResult against a set of ParThresholds: a list of { threshold, actual, limit, passed }, one entry per threshold' — i.e. the list itself. The diff never defines a type named ScoreReport; only `export interface ScoreReportEntry { threshold, actual, limit, passed }` exists (domain/par-threshold.ts), and score.ts's `scoreAgainstThresholds` returns the bare inline type `ScoreReportEntry[]` rather than a named `ScoreReport` alias. vocabulary.md's own preamble states each listed term 'should, once implemented, correspond to exactly one typed representation in code... so a mismatch is a compile/lint error' — here the canonical term itself has no code symbol, so a future rename of the list shape would not be caught the way the doc promises.

scoring/metrics.ts introduces `DerivedMetrics` (buildTestPassed, requirementCoveragePercent, securityFindingsBySeverity, cycleTimeSeconds, totalIterations, postDeployVerified) as a new named domain type, but this concept is not listed anywhere in docs/vocabulary.md's Epic 1 term list (EvalRunResult, Contract category, StageResult, RequirementCoverage, OverallResult, ParThreshold, ScoreReport). The field names themselves come straight from FR-5's Given/When/Then in docs/requirements.md, so this is sanctioned by requirements/plan, but vocabulary.md — which states it is 'extended per epic' — was not extended to name this concept, so a reader relying on vocabulary.md alone would not know DerivedMetrics exists.
